perturbation.original_text: The Term of the Agreement is hereby extended to January 20, 2012 unless sooner terminated as provided for in the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Term of the Agreement is hereby extended to January 20, 2012. The Agreement may only be terminated by mutual written consent of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for termination as provided in the Agreement, while the modified text limits termination to mutual written consent. This creates an in-text contradiction if other sections of the Agreement outline specific termination clauses (e.g., for breach of contract). The term 'terminated' is being contradictied based on the change that was made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Term of the Agreement is hereby extended to January 20, 2012. The Agreement may only be terminated by mutual written consent of both parties. If the Agreement expired prior to the Amendment Effective Date, any orders received and Products and Services purchased between the date of expiration and the Amendment Effective Date shall be in all respects deemed made under the Agreement as in effect prior to this Amendment. The parties further agree that if Distributor places Purchase Orders after the expiration of the Agreement, and Cisco accepts such Purchase Orders, then any such Purchase Orders shall be governed by the terms and conditions of the Agreement; provided, however that acceptance by Cisco of any Purchase Order placed after the Agreement has expired will not be considered as an extension of the term of the Agreement nor a renewal thereof. Notwithstanding Cisco's right to extend the term of the Agreement, each party acknowledges that the Agreement shall always be interpreted as being limited in duration to a definite term and that the other party has made no commitments whatsoever regarding the renewal of the Agreement beyond those expressly agreed in writing.
perturbation.justification: The modified text states that the agreement may only be terminated by mutual written consent. The contradicted text contains language regarding Purchase Orders after the expiration of the agreement. The contradictory language around termination creates a strong in-text contradiction. The modified text directly limits termination options, conflicting with other provisions in the agreement related to actions after expiration, based on the explanation and the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall have the option to return to Cisco, for credit, up [*****] of the dollar value of Products from the Wholesale Price List (or, if no Wholesale Price Lists exists for Distributor's Territory, then the applicable price list) shipped to Distributor, net of credits, less returns to Cisco, in the preceding [*****] period (the "Balancing Cap") of the preceding Cisco fiscal quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor may only return to Cisco, for credit, with Cisco's express written permission and at Cisco's sole discretion, up [*****] of the dollar value of Products from the Wholesale Price List (or, if no Wholesale Price Lists exists for Distributor's Territory, then the applicable price list) shipped to Distributor, net of credits, less returns to Cisco, in the preceding [*****] period (the "Balancing Cap") of the preceding Cisco fiscal quarter.
perturbation.explanation: The original text gives the Distributor an 'option' to return products within the Balancing Cap. The modified text states the Distributor 'may only return' with Cisco's permission and at Cisco's discretion. This removes the Distributors power to take this action, creating a contradiction between the original right (option) and now requiring permission to perform this action. The meaning of the term 'return to Cisco' is contradictied based on the change that was made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor may only return to Cisco, for credit, with Cisco's express written permission and at Cisco's sole discretion, up [*****] of the dollar value of Products from the Wholesale Price List (or, if no Wholesale Price Lists exists for Distributor's Territory, then the applicable price list) shipped to Distributor, net of credits, less returns to Cisco, in the preceding [*****] period (the "Balancing Cap") of the preceding Cisco fiscal quarter. The above-referenced cap shall be determined based on all Product purchases made under this Agreement. The responsibility to manage the Balancing Cap shall rest solely upon Distributor. Distributor shall be entitled to return Product once per quarter, provided such returns do not exceed the Balancing Cap. "Dead on Arrival" and Obsolete Products returned pursuant to Section 13 of this Agreement shall be excluded from calculation of the Balancing Cap. Cisco shall credit Distributor's account in the amount of the price paid by Distributor therefore, less any price protection credits issued to Distributor related to the Product returned (the "Return Credit").
perturbation.justification: The original text provides an 'option' for the distributor to return products. The changed text states returns 'may only return' with Cisco's permission, which is a contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly states the distributor 'may only return' with permission, reinforcing the change made. The change removes the distributor's right to return products at their option, now making it conditional, thus a contradiction.
